auth_notes: Requires creating a Service Account within the Mixpanel Project or Organization settings. Authentication uses the Service Account Username and Service Account Secret as the Basic Auth credentials. Note that Project Tokens are used for ingestion, while Service Accounts are required for the Query and Export APIs.
rate_limits: Query API: 60 requests per hour per project. Ingestion API: 2,000 events per second. Export API: 1 concurrent request per project. Rate limits vary significantly between the Data Ingestion and Data Querying APIs.
gotchas: Data Residency: Projects hosted in the EU must use 'api-eu.mixpanel.com' endpoints; using the standard US endpoints will result in 404 or auth errors.
Data Format: The Export API returns Newline Delimited JSON (NDJSON), which requires an extra step in n8n to split or parse into standard JSON objects.
Timezone handling: Mixpanel stores data in the project's timezone, which can cause discrepancies when querying via API if the n8n environment is set to UTC.
research_notes: The integration will primarily use the HTTP Request node. For tracking events, use POST to /track with the 'data' parameter Base64 encoded or as a JSON body. For data retrieval, the /export endpoint is most common but requires careful handling of large payloads and NDJSON formatting. Webhooks can be configured in Mixpanel under 'Integrations' to push data to an n8n Webhook node for real-time processing.
